articles.title: Oil: Ceasefire confusion keeps supply risks elevated – Danske Bank
articles.expand: Danske Research Team notes that Brent crude dropped sharply on the initial US-Iran ceasefire headlines but remains sensitive to Middle East supply risks. The Strait of Hormuz is still blocked, with Iran only indicating a possible reopening later this week.

articles.title: EUR/USD hesitates at 1.1660 with Iran’s ceasefire on tenterhooks

articles.title: USD: Focus on Inflation and Fed tensions risk – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Antje Praefcke argues that the Iran ceasefire is fragile and warns against expecting Oil prices to quickly return to pre-war levels. She notes that US inflation data, including CPI and the PCE index, are likely to reflect higher energy costs over time.

articles.title: Japan consumer sentiment plunges in March as US-Iran conflict weighs
articles.expand: The main consumer confidence index takes a dive from 39.7 in February to 33.3 in March. And that is the weakest reading since the middle of last year. The US-Iran conflict is threatening to derail the consumer recovery in Japan, not least with higher petrol prices and spillovers to higher inflation. And for the BOJ, it isn't the good kind of inflat...
